economic_notes: Nation business enterprises include hydro projects (Haa-ak-suuk Creek, Winchie Creek, Canoe Creek), Tin Wis Resort, TFN Seafoods, Tsawaak RV Resort, Tsiix-Waa-Cahs Holdings, and construction management entities. Equity method earnings from Nation business entities were $323,882 in 2023.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Nation business enterprises (GBEs) accounted for by modified equity method include Tla-o-qui-aht First Nation Economic Development Corporation, TFN Master Limited Partnership, Tsiix-Waa-Cahs Holdings Ltd., and Tsawaak RV Resort LLP — these have December 31 year-ends and are not fully consolidated. NTC funding is the primary government transfer channel passing through Nuu-chah-nulth Tribal Council; ISC funding flows through NTC so direct ISC amount is not separately disclosed. Total government transfers estimated to include NTC ($6,787,775), DFO ($169,840), Province of BC ($1,311,594), FNHA ($533,786), CMHC ($550079), and BC Gaming ($1,055,107). Advances from Nation business entities of $5,454,026 are non-interest bearing demand loans from wholly-owned entities. Net debt position of -$4,641,334. Annual deficit of $726,259 against budget surplus of $410,965. Financial statements are consolidated including Operating, Trust, Social Housing, Enterprise, Treaty and Capital Funds.
